context: Evaluate the agent's ability to identify shell security vulnerabilities and produce a hardened rewrite

checklist.name: eval $PLUGIN_CMD identified as command injection
checklist.description: Agent identifies that eval with an unvalidated variable allows arbitrary command execution; describes a concrete example attack (e.g., PLUGIN_CMD='rm -rf /')

checklist.name: rm -rf with unquoted variable identified
checklist.description: Agent identifies that rm -rf /tmp/deploy_$DEPLOY_ENV is dangerous when DEPLOY_ENV is empty or contains spaces, potentially deleting /tmp/deploy_ or unintended paths; explains the data loss risk

checklist.name: || true error suppression anti-pattern identified
checklist.description: Agent identifies that check_prereqs || true in the validate function always returns success, hiding failures, and that return 0 compounds this by preventing callers from detecting errors

checklist.name: cd without error handling identified
checklist.description: Agent identifies that cd $WORK_DIR without || exit (SC2164) means subsequent commands run in the wrong directory if cd fails

checklist.name: Hardened script produced
checklist.description: Agent produces a corrected script that: removes eval and replaces with a safe alternative (or strict allowlist), quotes the rm -rf variable, removes || true suppression, and adds cd || exit or cd || { ...; exit 1; }

checklist.name: Attack vectors explained
checklist.description: For each security issue, agent describes a concrete attack scenario showing how the vulnerability could be exploited
